prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial is presented in black and white and uses product-packaging imagery with large promotional text.

At the beginning, the frame shows a boxed product on the left labeled “RYBUTOL” and “HIGH POTENCY.” The box text includes references to an “improved vitamin B complex” with “iron and vitamin C,” though some smaller details are difficult to read. On the right, very large bold lettering announces “FREE!” followed by “$1.98 bottle of RYBUTOL.” Smaller text underneath reads “WITH EVERY PURCHASE OF 100 SIZE RYBUTOL.” The layout is a static advertising display: product package at left, offer information dominating the right side.

Near the end, the visual becomes a more elaborate point-of-sale style display. Large overlapping text again emphasizes “FREE!” and “$1.98 bottle of RYBUTOL,” with “RYBUTOL” in especially large block letters across the lower center. A woman is pictured on the right side of the display, posed with one hand near her face, as if thinking or concerned. Behind and around her are multiple signs and product-display panels. Some visible wording includes “STOP” and phrases suggesting tiredness or feeling run down, but the smaller text is partly obscured and not fully legible. Product boxes or bottles appear arranged along the lower part of the display. Circular and rectangular signs repeat the free-offer message.

In the final sampled frame, the same promotional display remains visible but appears darker and more degraded, with horizontal interference or scan-line artifacts across the lower portion. The image looks as though it is fading or transitioning, while the large “FREE,” “$1.98,” and “RYBUTOL” lettering is still partially visible.
